conversations.content: Today we studied the Stellar Ring Technology Group Data Security Emergency Plan. If the internal network encounters quantum-level computational power cracking...
Quantum computing cracking? What kind of hacker is this! How do you respond?
then activate the honeypot system for reverse tracking.
Honeypot reverse tracking — turn the tables! Any other security threats?
If abnormal data outflow from the core R&D repository occurs, then physically fuse the external optical cable connections.
Data outflow — physically fuse the optical cables, cut off completely! Third scenario?
If an AI model undergoes uncontrollable self-iteration, then disconnect the neural network training cluster.
AI uncontrollable self-iteration? Disconnect the training cluster, force shutdown! Is there a fourth?
If an employee's brain-computer interface is hacked, then forcibly release an electromagnetic pulse to sever the connection.
Brain-computer interface hacked — electromagnetic pulse to block connection, protect the person! Understood!

think: Stellar Ring Technology data security: four threats: quantum-level cracking of internal network → activate honeypot reverse tracking; abnormal outflow from core R&D repository → physically fuse external optical cables; uncontrollable AI self-iteration → disconnect neural network training cluster; employee BCI hacked → forcibly release electromagnetic pulse to sever connection.
memory: Stellar Ring Technology Group Data Security Emergency Plan: If the internal network encounters quantum-level computational power cracking, then activate the honeypot system for reverse tracking; if abnormal data outflow from the core R&D repository occurs, then physically fuse the external optical cable connections; if an AI model undergoes uncontrollable self-iteration, then disconnect the neural network training cluster; if an employee's brain-computer interface is hacked, then forcibly release an electromagnetic pulse to sever the connection.
